heading: Public access to information
label: 2217 Public access to information
notes: Amendments2013— substituted “Administrator of FEMA” for “Director” in two places in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 112–2392000— substituted “Director” for “Secretary” in two places in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 106–503

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of all functions, personnel, assets, components, authorities, grant programs, and liabilities of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, including the functions of the Under Secretary for Federal Emergency Management relating thereto, to the Federal Emergency Management Agency, see , Domestic Security.section 315(a)(1) of Title 6For transfer of functions, personnel, assets, and liabilities of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, including the functions of the Director of the Federal Emergency Management Agency relating thereto, to the Secretary of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see former section 313(1) and sections 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

Transfer of FunctionsFunctions of National Fire Prevention and Control Administration [now United States Fire Administration] and National Academy for Fire Prevention and Control generally transferred to Federal Emergency Management Agency. For further details see Transfer of Functions note set out under .section 2202 of this title
amendments: Amendments2013— substituted “Administrator of FEMA” for “Director” in two places in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 112–2392000— substituted “Director” for “Secretary” in two places in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 106–503

children.text: upon request, to other Federal Government departments and agencies for official use;
upon request, to any committee of Congress having jurisdiction over the subject matter to which the information relates;
in any judicial proceeding under a court order formulated to preserve the confidentiality of such information without impairing the proceedings; and

to the public when he determines such disclosure to be necessary in order to protect health and safety after notice and opportunity for comment in writing or for discussion in closed session within fifteen days by the party to which the information pertains (if the delay resulting from such notice and opportunity for comment would not be detrimental to health and safety).
